compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: A+B+C implemented on PR #66 (commit ec85e4cd5) and verified end-to-end. The original container-RuntimeError fix (commit a5dec5f60) restored the agentic Pass-1 tier, which exposed two latent failures — both fixed: (C) agentic ticket tools read a separate read-only PINNED copy of the ticket store materialized from the 'tickets' ref into <store>/tickets-<sha>/.tickets-tracker/ (new _active_tickets_root contextvar + cfg.tickets_path, mirroring the code-root mechanism; code snapshot untouched/attestation-faithful); (B) read-only ticket contract (comment withheld when reading a pinned snapshot); (A) UsageLimits(tool_calls_limit) backstop so a failing/looping tool can't burn the budget (pydantic-ai #2593). Design validated against pydantic-ai v1.107 idioms (deps-injected pinned root; tool_calls_limit) + peers (OpenAI Agents SDK read-only sandboxes, LangGraph checkpoints). Verified: review-plan on epic 5ca8 now logs 0 'container bin FAILED', 0 'cannot list .tickets-tracker' (was 26), 0 step-budget exhaustions (was 2); container tier runs a real agentic call; verdict PASS (was INDETERMINATE), 21 llm_calls. 291 plan-review/store tests pass.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Correction: the A+B+C fixes are in PR #67 (not #66). PR #66 (MERGED to main) = the primary container-RuntimeError fix (a5dec5f60). PR #67 (commit ec85e4cd5) = the two exposed-issue fixes + tool-call backstop. NOTE: until #67 merges, main has the agentic-tier restore WITHOUT the ticket-store pin, so review-plan on an epic returns INDETERMINATE (agentic tier runs but hits the .tickets-tracker errors). #67 closes that window.

compiled_state.description:
The plan-review Pass-1 thread fan-out loses the active gate session, so every agentic finder call dispatched to a worker thread fails before any LLM call and its findings are dropped — the agentic (tool-using) review tier is silently dead in the fan-out while the gate still returns PASS.

## Reproduction Steps
1. Run `rebar review-plan <epic-with-children>` (the gate wraps the run in a `gate_session()` on the main thread).
2. Observe stderr: `WARNING rebar.llm.plan_review.pass1: container bin [...] FAILED in 0.0s (RuntimeError)` on every run.
3. The container bin "fails" in 0.0s (before any LLM call); the agentic Pass-1 chunks fail the same way but are swallowed silently by `sizing.pass1_with_ladder` (`return []`).

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: the agentic container/chunk finders run inside the gate session and produce findings.
- Actual: `assert_gated("agentic filesystem tools")` (runner.py:204, pre-LLM) raises `RuntimeError` in the worker because `_in_gate_session` (a `contextvars.ContextVar`) is NOT inherited by raw `ThreadPoolExecutor` threads (inherited by asyncio tasks only — documented in config.py). Findings are dropped; the gate degrades to single_turn + deterministic coverage and still returns PASS.

## Root Cause
`pass1.py` submits agentic `runner.run` work to a raw `ThreadPoolExecutor` at two sites (`_run_container` and `run_pass1`'s chunk pool) without propagating the calling thread's context. Proven by a no-LLM experiment + a RED test reproducing the exact warning.

## Fix
`_submit_ctx(ex, fn, *args)` submitting `ex.submit(contextvars.copy_context().run, fn, *args)` (a FRESH context copy per task) at both fan-out sites. Regression test: `tests/interfaces/store/test_pass1_fanout_gate_session.py`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Agentic Pass-1 finder calls dispatched to fan-out worker threads observe the active gate session (no `assert_gated` RuntimeError).
- [ ] `rebar review-plan` on an epic logs no `container bin ... FAILED ... (RuntimeError)` and the container tier produces findings.
- [ ] Regression test `test_pass1_fanout_gate_session.py` is RED before / GREEN after; existing gate-threading tests still pass.
- [ ] Fix lands via PR to main.
compiled_state.title: plan-review Pass-1 thread fan-out loses gate session — agentic tier silently dead
